claims.claim: Member roles are listed as objects required by content items/pages that are NOT included in content synchronization (i.e., must be pre-existing on target)
claims.location: Section: Ensure compatibility between instances, bullet list item (line 188)
claims.evidence: MemberRoleCompatibilityEvaluator.cs exists as an ICompatibilityEvaluator implementation (line 14-18 assembly registration). The Evaluate() method (line 46-79) checks member roles on the target and returns errors if missing or mismatched — confirming member roles are validated for compatibility but NOT transferred by content sync itself.
claims.docFact: AREA-014: MemberRoleCompatibilityEvaluator already existed on master

claims.claim: Member role definitions must exist on all connected instances if content items use role-based access restrictions
claims.location: Section: Ensure compatibility between instances, bullet list item (line 188)
claims.evidence: MemberRoleCompatibilityEvaluator.Evaluate() (line 60-76): when a source member role report has no matching GUID on the target, an error string is generated via localizationService.GetString('synchronization.compatibility.memberrole.notfound'). This produces a CompatibilityResult failure, blocking sync. The Create() method (line 32-42) collects only member roles referenced in the sync node graph (i.e., those used by content items), so the check is conditional on usage.
claims.docFact: AREA-014: MemberRoleCompatibilityEvaluator already existed on master

claims.claim: The system matches member roles between instances by their GUID and code name
claims.location: Section: Ensure compatibility between instances, bullet list item (line 188)
claims.evidence: MemberRoleCompatibilityEvaluator.Evaluate() line 53-57: queries target member roles by MemberRoleGUID (.WhereIn(nameof(MemberRoleInfo.MemberRoleGUID), reports.Select(r => r.Guid))). Line 61-67: if no GUID match found → 'notfound' error. Line 68-73: if GUID matches but MemberRoleName differs → 'namemismatch' error (using CompatibilityEvaluatorConstants.StringComparison). Both GUID and code name must be compatible.
claims.docFact: AREA-014: MemberRoleCompatibilityEvaluator already existed on master

claims.claim: An error will occur if you try to synchronize a content item for which an exactly matching member role is not available on the target
claims.location: Section: Ensure compatibility between instances, paragraph after bullet list (line 190)
claims.evidence: MemberRoleCompatibilityEvaluator.Evaluate() returns CompatibilityResult.From(errors) (line 78) where errors include 'notfound' for missing GUIDs and 'namemismatch' for code name mismatches. Non-null error strings produce a failed CompatibilityResult, which blocks the synchronization operation.
claims.docFact: AREA-014: MemberRoleCompatibilityEvaluator already existed on master

claims.claim: Content access settings are synchronized for content items and pages
claims.location: Section: Content sync internals, bullet point (line 236)
claims.evidence: IContentAccessSettingsEvaluator is consumed by multiple restore actions: (1) CreateContentItemRestoreAction.cs line 24,77,86 — uses ResolveContentAccessSettings and sets AccessSettings on content item creation. (2) UpdateContentItemMetadataRestoreAction.cs line 22,46,50 — resolves and applies access settings during content item metadata updates. (3) CreateWebPageRestoreAction.cs line 26,73,157 — applies access settings during web page creation. (4) UpdateWebPageItemUpdateSecuritySettingsRestoreAction.cs line 24,40-43,54 — resolves and applies access settings during web page security updates. Both content items (reusable) and web pages are covered.
claims.docFact: AREA-014: ContentItemMemberRoleAdapter and MemberRoleAdapter already existed on master

claims.claim: If a content item is secured with member role restrictions on the source instance, the same security configuration and role assignments are restored on the target instance
claims.location: Section: Content sync internals, bullet point (line 236)
claims.evidence: ContentAccessSettingsEvaluator.ResolveContentAccessSettings() (lines 26-57): (1) Checks sourceContentItem.ContentItemIsSecured — if false, returns ContentAccessSettings.Public(). (2) If secured, resolves member role GUIDs from sync node dependencies (ContentItemMemberRoleInfo → MemberRoleInfo chain via lines 37-44). (3) If no roles found, returns ContentAccessSettings.Secured() (any-authenticated). (4) If roles found, queries target MemberRoleInfo by GUID to get local role IDs (lines 51-54), returns new ContentAccessSettings(true, roleIds). This resolved ContentAccessSettings is then passed to manager.UpdateMetadata() or manager.UpdateSecuritySettings(), faithfully reproducing the source security configuration on the target.
claims.docFact: AREA-014: ContentItemMemberRoleAdapter and MemberRoleAdapter already existed on master

claims.claim: Member role bindings are discovered as content item dependencies in sync payloads (implicit from dependency adapter chain)
claims.location: Section: Content sync internals, bullet point (line 236) — implicit in 'role assignments are restored'
claims.evidence: ContentItemMemberRoleAdapter.cs implements IDependencyAdapter<ContentItemInfo> (line 19) and retrieves ContentItemMemberRoleInfo records linked to a content item (lines 22-26). MemberRoleAdapter.cs implements IDependencyAdapter<ContentItemMemberRoleInfo> (line 19) and resolves the actual MemberRoleInfo for each binding (lines 22-28). This two-step adapter chain (ContentItem → ContentItemMemberRole → MemberRole) ensures member role bindings are included in the sync dependency graph.
claims.docFact: AREA-014: ContentItemMemberRoleAdapter and MemberRoleAdapter already existed on master
